category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}{{jalink|Minamisōma|Q642094}}{{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{jalink|Takakuraji|Q11668757|Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{jalink|April 20|Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Haramachi Ward|Q24892679}}, {{jalink|Minamisōma|Q642094}}, {{jalink|Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{jalink|Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takakuraji|Q11668757|Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{jalink|Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{jalink|806|Q30021}} ({{jalink|Daidō|Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{jalink|Bun'an|Q1193586}} ({{jalink|1444|Q6420}}-{{jalink|1449|Q6446}}), lord {{jalink|Sōma clan|Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{jalink|1602|Q6727}} ({{jalink|Keichō|Q1328185}} 7), lord {{jalink|Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{jalink|Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{jalink|Genbun|Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{jalink|1774|Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{jalink|1799|Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{jalink|1815|Q6974}} ({{jalink|Bunka|Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{jalink|Sōma Masatane|Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6380005007071 == Commons category (P373) == * Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区押釜前田277', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446|1=1449}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379|1=Sōma clan}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane (daimyo, born 1548)}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697|1=1774}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827|1=1799}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974|1=Human herpesvirus 6}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|1=Bunka}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6380005007071 == Commons category (P373) == * Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区押釜前田277', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446|1=1449}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379|1=Sōma clan}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane (daimyo, born 1548)}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697|1=1774}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827|1=1799}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974|1=Human herpesvirus 6}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|1=Bunka}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6380005007071 == Commons category (P373) == * Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区押釜前田277', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446|1=1449}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379|1=Sōma clan}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane (daimyo, born 1548)}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697|1=1774}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827|1=1799}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974|1=Human herpesvirus 6}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|1=Bunka}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかくらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6380005007071 == Commons category (P373) == * Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区押釜前田277', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446|1=1449}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379|1=Sōma clan}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane (daimyo, born 1548)}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697|1=1774}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827|1=1799}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974|1=Human herpesvirus 6}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|1=Bunka}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6380005007071 == Commons category (P373) == * Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県南相馬市原町区押釜前田277', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takakura-jinja (Minamisoma)}} [[JA:高座神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11669904}} == Japanese content == {{混同|x1=for the one in Yao, Osaka|Amanoteru Ōkami Takakura Shrine and Iwato Shrine|x2=for the one in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi|Takakuramusubi Miko Shrine}} {{神社 |名称=Takakura Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}{{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}} Oshikama Maeda 277 |位置={{Coord|37|37|36.1|N|140|55|15.1|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takakura Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
(trad.){{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}
(trad.){{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Takakura Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takakura Shrine''' (たかくらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Haramachi Ward|Q24892679|WD=Q24892679|1=Haramachi-ku}}, {{ill|lt=Minamisōma|Q642094|WD=Q642094|1=Minamisōma}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|WD=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. Its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === * It enshrines {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}. * According to the Sōma-domain historical work ''Ōsōshi'', it enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Takakuraji|Q11668757|WD=Q11668757|lt=Kumano Takakuraji-no-Mikoto|1=Takakuraji}}, and {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. ** Another theory says it enshrined Izanami-no-Mikoto, Yomotsuchi-moribito, and {{ill|lt=Kukurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|1=Kukurihime}}, and was called “Hakusan Sansho Gongen.” * According to records, the sacred object is “five sun” in length. === Origin === According to ''Ōsōshi'', it is said to be one of the eight shikinaisha shrines in Namekata District among the 100 shikinaisha shrines in Mutsu Province, though details of its enshrinement history are unknown. In ancient times it is said to have stood in Takakura village west of the current site (present Takakura, Haramachi Ward), with broad shrine grounds and many precinct shrines. A former site remains near Akashiba in Baba, Haramachi Ward, at the boundary of Oshikama, Baba, and Ōkido districts. The shrine grounds are thought to have become desolate from war and conflict; though rebuilt, it did not fully recover and became a small shrine. On April 20, {{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}} ({{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 1), shrine buildings were constructed, and during {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}-{{ill|lt=1449|Q6446|WD=Q6446|1=1449}}), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma clan|Q2975379|WD=Q2975379|1=Sōma clan}} donated one kan and eighteen mon of shrine rice-field income. In {{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7), lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Yoshitane (16th head)|Q7559115|WD=Q7559115|lt=Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane (daimyo, born 1548)}} was dispossessed by order of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and those donated lands were lost. The shrine steward at that time was young and could not respond, and the lands remained lost thereafter. Trees in the shrine area were also cut and turned into fields, and by the {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|WD=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} period those lands had become荒廃. Before that, a man named Endō Hachirōzaemon lived in the village and set up a small shrine at the north end of the old grounds as a guardian deity. His heir and descendants are said to have lived around present Oshikama. In {{ill|lt=1774|Q7697|WD=Q7697|1=1774}} (An'ei 3), the shrine was rebuilt, and a ridge tag from that time remains. In {{ill|lt=1799|Q7827|WD=Q7827|1=1799}} (Kansei 11), a field fire burned the shrine buildings. At that time the domain gave 11 ryō 2 bu obtained by selling several hundred cedar trees from the grounds, and the shrine was restored. In {{ill|lt=1815|Q6974|WD=Q6974|1=Human herpesvirus 6}} ({{ill|lt=Bunka|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|1=Bunka}} 12), by order of lord {{ill|lt=Sōma Masatane|Q8514605|WD=Q8514605|1=Sōma Masutane}}, a survey identified this shrine as “Takakura Shrine,” one of the eight Namekata shikinaisha shrines. In that year, one koku of rice field was lent as shrine land in recognition of its history. === Precincts === * The shrine buildings stand on a hill beside a river flowing through Oshikama. * Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 01.JPG|In front of Takakura Shrine torii File:Torii gate of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 02.JPG|Torii File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Approach File:Main approach to Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|View of shrine buildings from the approach File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 1.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Haiden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward 2.JPG|Takakura Shrine haiden File:Honden of Takakura-jinja shrine in Haramachi ward.JPG|Takakura Shrine honden
Inside the protective structure is a kibabuki nagare-zukuri honden === References === * ''Ōsōshi'' (Sōma City History 4), Sōma City, 1969 === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takakura Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:南相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:南相馬市の建築物]]
